metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Co-locate the protocol for how `ProductionBatchRunner` threads `repo_root` into project-criterion descriptor resolution — specify whether `_resolve_criteria` gains a `repo_root` parameter or whether the runner performs a separate two-phase lookup (YAML-included + project-appended) and document the dedup rule.
findings.finding: The decision rule for how `_resolve_criteria` (or its replacement) accesses `repo_root` to look up project-criterion descriptors is not co-located with the function's call site. The plan says `ProductionBatchRunner` appends project descriptors from `route_criteria(ctx)`, but `_resolve_criteria` currently receives only a `criteria: tuple[Mapping]` (the static YAML batch list) — it has no `repo_root` parameter. The plan's prose about the runner 'appending ONLY the project.<name>-prefixed descriptors' implies a second lookup against `effective_criteria(repo_root)` inside the runner, but the call-site protocol (who passes `repo_root`, whether `_resolve_criteria` is extended or replaced, whether the project subset is resolved before or after the YAML criteria are resolved) is never stated.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `repo_root: str | None = None` to `_resolve_criteria`; after resolving the YAML-included set via `registry.by_id()`, call `effective_criteria(repo_root)` to get the full set and append any `project.`-prefixed descriptors not already in `seen`, tier-split by `exec_tier`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the cross-module cache-invalidation coupling: define whether `registry.py` exposes a `clear_overlay_caches()` hook that `prompt_library._invalidate_caches()` calls, and confirm no circular-import path exists; add this contract to ADR 0015.
findings.finding: The mechanism by which `prompt_library._invalidate_caches()` reaches the new module-level dicts in `registry.py` is unspecified, risking an undocumented cross-module coupling or a silent circular-import dependency.
findings.location: Cache strategy section / `prompt_library._invalidate_caches()` bullet; Acceptance Criteria (remediation) last bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Have `registry.py` expose a `clear_overlay_caches()` module-level function (clears the `(repo_root, sha256)` keyed dict and any related lru_caches); `prompt_library._invalidate_caches()` calls it via a direct import in one direction only. Document the call direction in ADR 0015 to prevent future inversion.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Consider extracting at least two self-contained child stories from the root infrastructure: (a) CI drift/parity gate + ADR (AC5, AC6 — purely validating/documenting artefacts), and (b) the cache-isolation RED test harness (AC4 remediation — requires independent MCP-server simulation); leave the runtime overlay merge + fan-out as the true MVP root.
findings.finding: The root/infrastructure story carries 12 acceptance criteria (7 original + 5 remediation) across 6+ files and 3+ layers, yet no child stories decompose the infrastructure itself — only downstream consumers (#2, #4, #6) are children. Per rubric G5, >6 ACs on a parent story signals it should have children that decompose its own work, not just its consumers.
findings.suggested_fix: Split into: Story A (MVP root, ~7 ACs): overlay schema, effective_criteria, fan-out, sanitization, missing-prompt error, additive signatures, e2e demo. Story B (observability, ~2 ACs): ADR + CI parity gate. Story C (cache hardening, ~2 ACs): content-hash memo, cross-repo RED test, _invalidate_caches. Stories B and C can begin in parallel once Story A's interfaces are stable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly document in the `plan_review_assemble_criteria` op (and/or the ADR) that `include_project_<name>` outputs are **coverage/observability-only** — not active YAML routing gates — because the immutable YAML cannot reference them; project criteria routing is entirely runner-controlled via `route_criteria`.
findings.finding: The `include_project_<name>` booleans emitted by `plan_review_assemble_criteria` have no YAML `when:` consumer (the YAML is immutable and unchanged), making them coverage/observability outputs only — but the plan does not explicitly state this distinction. An implementer who reads 'assemble still emits sanitized include_project_<name> booleans' alongside the built-in pattern (where include_<ID> IS the active YAML gate) could mistakenly treat the project booleans as active routing gates, attempt to reference them in the YAML, or expect that setting one to false prevents the criterion from running. Because project criteria are entirely runner-controlled (appended by ProductionBatchRunner after resolving route_criteria), the assemble output has no effect on whether a project criterion runs — only the runner's own re-application of route_criteria does.
findings.location: What section / Dynamic Pass-1 fan-out mechanism / plan_review_assemble_criteria (workflow_ops.py:165) and ProductionBatchRunner (production_batch_runner.py)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a doc comment in `plan_review_assemble_criteria` adjacent to the project-criteria include_ emission: '# NOTE: include_project_<name> outputs are coverage/routing-record only — the immutable gates/plan-review.yaml has no when: entry for them. Project criteria routing is controlled entirely by ProductionBatchRunner.run() via route_criteria, not by these booleans.' Mirror this distinction in ADR 0015.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name `attest.registry_version()` explicitly in the ADR and/or the story #4 dependency as a site that must incorporate the overlay's activated criteria + routing into the version stamp; add a `# TODO(story-4): include effective_criteria(repo_root) in the hash` comment at `attest.py:registry_version()` so story #4 finds the gap.
findings.finding: `registry_version()` in `attest.py` stamps only `CANONICAL_LLM` + `_routing_index()` (the packaged sets). After this story, the overlay's activated project criteria and their routing are not included in the registry version stamp, so adding/removing/changing a project criterion does NOT invalidate existing attestations. The plan defers attestation invalidation to story #4, but does not explicitly identify `registry_version()` as a known gap for the overlay — leaving it implicit that story #4 must patch this site.
findings.location: Scope section / 'Attestation invalidation is story #4' / src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py:37-57

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly document at the `plan_review_assemble_criteria` op (or in the ADR) that `include_project_<name>` keys are routing-record/coverage signals only and do NOT gate the runner's project fan-out — the runner appends project criteria unconditionally (subject to `applies()`/overlay filter already applied by `route_criteria`).
findings.finding: The role of the `include_project_<name>` boolean emitted by `plan_review_assemble_criteria` is ambiguous at the consume site. The plan says the key is emitted for 'coverage/routing record' purposes, but it never states that the key does NOT gate the project fan-out in the YAML `when:` expressions (because the YAML is immutable). An implementer could reasonably expect the `include_project_foo` output to be consumed by a `when:` expression in the YAML batch step — mirroring the built-in `include_E1` pattern — but no such entry can exist (YAML is UNTOUCHED per the plan). The decision rule 'project criteria are always appended by the runner; `include_project_<name>` is observability only' is not stated at either the assemble or consume site.
findings.location: Plan § 'Dynamic Pass-1 fan-out mechanism'; AC line: '`include_<id>` key sanitization (`.`→`_`) applied at both assemble + consume sites'; `workflow_ops.plan_review_assemble_criteria`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the clearing rule for the new dict-based `effective_routing`/overlay memos in `_invalidate_caches()`: state whether it calls `dict.clear()` on the module-level dict (clearing all repos) or requires a `repo_root` parameter for targeted eviction, and document the chosen tradeoff.
findings.finding: The clearing protocol for the new module-level dict-based memo (`effective_routing` / `load_criteria` repo-keyed dicts) inside `prompt_library._invalidate_caches()` is unspecified. The plan says these are 'memoized in repo-keyed module dicts keyed by (repo_root, sha256(overlay-bytes))' and that `_invalidate_caches()` 'clears both memos + the lru_caches.' But the current `_invalidate_caches` only calls `.cache_clear()` on lru_cache objects; a plain dict requires either `dict.clear()` (evicts all repos simultaneously) or targeted key removal (requires enumerating keys). Neither approach is stated, and the two have different semantics in a long-lived MCP server serving multiple repos.
findings.location: Plan § 'Cache strategy — content-hash, not mtime'; src/rebar/llm/prompt_library.py `_invalidate_caches` (lines 230–237)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a module-level `_overlay_memo: dict = {}` in `registry.py` and have `_invalidate_caches()` call `registry._overlay_memo.clear()` — clearing all repos on any authoring write, which is safe since the cache is a performance optimization only.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that the overlay 'reuses the packaged routing schema' only at the per-criterion entry level (exec/applies_at/etc.), not at the top-level envelope: update the What section and the ADR schema doc to specify the overlay's full top-level structure (`{plan_review: {<id>: <routing-entry>}, activate: [...]}`) as distinct from the packaged flat `{<id>: <routing-entry>}` format, so project authors know exactly what to write.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the `.rebar/criteria_routing.json` overlay 'reuses the packaged routing schema,' but the overlay's described top-level structure (`{plan_review: {...}, activate: [...]}`) is structurally different from the existing packaged `criteria_routing.json`, which is a flat `{criterion-id: routing-entry}` map with no gate-key and no `activate` list. The claim that the overlay 'reuses' the packaged schema is an unverified assertion: at most the per-entry sub-object (exec/applies_at/block_threshold/etc.) is shared, but the envelope is wholly new. A project author consulting the 'reuses packaged routing schema' claim will not know whether to write a flat file or a gate-keyed one.
findings.location: ## What — '.rebar/criteria_routing.json overlay: gate-keyed (`{plan_review: {...}, activate: [...]}`), reusing the packaged routing schema'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'reusing the packaged routing schema' with 'each per-criterion entry in the overlay uses the same routing sub-schema as the packaged `criteria_routing.json` entries (exec/applies_at/block_threshold/checklist); the overlay envelope is new: `{"plan_review": {"<project.name>": <routing-entry>, ...}, "activate": ["project.<name>", ...]}`'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Retract or explicitly supersede the 'uncached, or keyed by (repo_root, overlay mtime)' wording in the **What** section so that the content-hash-keyed memo strategy from the remediation section is the single authoritative specification.
findings.finding: The 'What' section describes `effective_routing(repo_root)` as '(uncached, or keyed by (repo_root, overlay mtime) — note mtime granularity, prefer content hash if flaky)', explicitly leaving both 'uncached' and 'mtime-keyed' as live options. The remediation section closes this to 'content signature, NOT mtime' — but the 'What' section text is never retracted. An implementer reading top-to-bottom encounters two conflicting specifications for the same function's caching strategy.
findings.location: ## What — Cache isolation bullet vs. ## Backward-compatibility … ## Cache strategy — content-hash, not mtime
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'What' section, replace 'add `effective_routing(repo_root)` (uncached, or keyed by `(repo_root, overlay mtime)` — note mtime granularity, prefer content hash if flaky)' with 'add `effective_routing(repo_root)`, memoized in a module-level dict keyed by `(repo_root, sha256(overlay-bytes))` — see Cache Strategy section for rationale.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile AC4's 'overlay is not [cached]' with the body's content-hash memo description: either rewrite AC4 to say 'overlay is content-hash-memoized per repo_root (not a global lru_cache)' or explicitly state 'overlay is read fresh on every call (no memo)' and remove the content-hash memo from the body.
findings.finding: AC4 states 'packaged index stays cached; overlay is not' but the body's cache-strategy section states the overlay IS memoized (in a repo-keyed dict keyed by `(repo_root, sha256(overlay-bytes))`). These are contradictory descriptions of the same mechanism, which will mislead implementors about whether overlay results are cached.
findings.location: AC4 ('overlay is not [cached]') vs. 'Cache strategy' section ('memoized in repo-keyed module dicts keyed by (repo_root, sha256(overlay-bytes))')
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite AC4 to: 'Packaged index stays `@lru_cache`d (immutable per binary); overlay results are memoized in a per-process dict keyed by `(repo_root, sha256(overlay-bytes))` — not a global lru_cache — so cross-repo leakage is impossible and a content change auto-invalidates; `_invalidate_caches()` clears this dict.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete bound/eviction mechanism for the module-level memo dict (e.g., `functools.lru_cache` with a max-size, or an LRU dict) and document who triggers `_invalidate_caches()` — or under what condition — in the long-lived MCP server to retire stale entries.
findings.finding: The plan claims stale-entry growth in the module-level memo dict is 'bounded' but provides no mechanism for the bound. In a long-lived MCP server processing many repos (or a repo whose overlay is edited repeatedly), each unique `(repo_root, sha256)` pair adds a permanent entry to the dict; `_invalidate_caches()` is the only cleanup path but the plan does not specify who calls it, at what frequency, or under what trigger in the MCP server process.
findings.location: Cache strategy section: 'stale-entry growth is bounded: prompt_library._invalidate_caches() clears both memos + the lru_caches'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the plain module-level dict with a bounded LRU (e.g., `functools.lru_cache(maxsize=64)` or a small `collections.OrderedDict`-based LRU keyed by `(repo_root, sha256)`). Document the eviction policy in the ADR. If `_invalidate_caches()` is the primary path, specify the MCP server hook (e.g., file-watch event or periodic TTL) that calls it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the CI drift/parity gate's artifact form and location (e.g., 'a pytest test in `tests/test_registry_drift.py` asserting schema-validity and CANONICAL_LLM parity, run by the existing `ci/lint` job') so AC5 is unambiguously evaluable in-session.
findings.finding: AC5 ('Routing validation: invalid overlay routing is a located error + a CI drift/parity gate') and the corresponding 'What' bullet ('a CI drift/parity gate mirroring reviewers/index.json') do not specify what artifact constitutes the gate — CI config file, a pytest test module, a pre-commit hook — leaving the implementer to choose the form and location.
findings.location: ## Acceptance Criteria AC5; ## What — Routing validation bullet; ## Scope clarification

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the server-side behavior when overlay JSON is malformed/partial at read time: either (a) retain the last-known-good cached entry and surface a warning (not hard error) for transient parse failures, or (b) require callers to write the overlay atomically (write-to-temp + `os.replace`) and document this as a contract in the ADR.
findings.finding: The plan does not address the partial-write safety of `.rebar/criteria_routing.json` in a long-lived MCP server. An editor saving the file in two flushes (or a `git checkout` mid-write) can cause the server to read a truncated/partial JSON file. The plan says this surfaces as a located `RegistryError`, but it does not specify whether the server retries with the previously valid cached entry or immediately propagates the error to the in-flight plan-review request.
findings.location: Cache strategy section + AC: 'An activated project criterion with a missing/malformed prompt file raises a LOCATED RegistryError'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Consolidate: move the collision/rebind rejection test requirement into Remediation AC4 (the enumerated test list) and trim AC2 to say only 'overlay per schema: gate-keyed, `project.<name>` namespace, explicit `activate` list, load-time error for schema violations' — leaving error-case test enumeration solely to Remediation AC4.
findings.finding: AC2 and Remediation AC4 both require 'collision/rebind rejection (located error)' as a verifiable condition, making this a near-duplicate criterion split across two AC lists.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend AC1) to explicitly state: '`route_criteria(ctx)` receives `ctx.repo_root` and forwards it to `effective_criteria`; a unit test with two distinct `repo_root` values confirms each receives its own criterion set.'
findings.finding: The plan body (Scope clarification) names `orchestrator.route_criteria` threading `repo_root` as an explicit deliverable, but no acceptance criterion verifies this contract directly; AC1's 'fans out and runs' only proves end-to-end behavior, not the intermediate contract.
findings.location: Scope clarification section: 'orchestrator.route_criteria (route_criteria threads repo_root)' — no matching AC

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
